title: Pokémon - I Choose You!
overview: Meet Ash Ketchum, a 10-year-old boy full of imagination, confidence, and dreams that are almost too big for his village of Pallet Town—plus the desire to become a Pokémon Master!

title: Pokémon Emergency!
overview: Ash rushes into Viridian City with his gravely wounded Pikachu. The city's on high alert for Pokémon thieves-thieves like Jessie, James, and Meowth of Team Rocket, a group of bad guys dedicated to stealing valuable Pokémon.

title: Ash Catches a Pokémon
overview: Ash discovers and catches a Caterpie—his first Pokémon capture! Though Ash is excited, Misty is grossed out by Bug-type Pokémon, so she is repulsed by its attempts to be friends with her. At night, as Ash and Misty sleep, Caterpie shares with Pikachu its dream of evolving into a Butterfree.

title: Challenge of the Samurai
overview: Misty and Ash continue to wander the Viridian Forest when they spot a Weedle. As Ash battles it, he's distracted by a boy dressed as a samurai and calling himself (naturally) Samurai. Samurai challenges Ash to a battle, and Ash gets the advantage when his Metapod defends itself against Samurai's Pinsir. Samurai sends out his own Metapod—and a standoff begins.

title: Showdown in Pewter City
overview: Jessie, James, and Meowth dig a trap for our heroes, but they end up falling into it themselves! Ash and Misty arrive at Pewter City and meet a curious fellow named Flint, who notices that Pikachu is tired and shows them the way to the Pokémon Center.

title: Clefairy and the Moon Stone
overview: Will Team Rocket steal the Moon Stone? Are there other Clefairy lurking about, and do they carry Moon Stones, too? And is the legend of the Moon Stone true?

title: The Water Flowers of Cerulean City
overview: Ash seeks the Cascade badge in Cerulean City—but for some reason, Misty doesn't want to join them. When Ash and Brock show up, the city is buzzing about a break-in that just took place—and are initially accused of the crime!

title: The Path to the Pokémon League
overview: On the way to Vermillion City, Ash meets AJ, a Gym Leader at an unsanctioned Gym who has 98 wins and zero losses. They battle, and Ash is surprised that AJ's Ground-type Sandshrew can easily beat his Flying-type Pokémon.

title: The School of Hard Knocks
overview: While searching for firewood in the foggy woods, Ash and Pikachu discover a bunch of prep-school kids bullying a classmate named Joe.

title: Bulbasaur and the Hidden Village
overview: Ash and Misty, while arguing, discover an Oddish. As Misty's Starmie weakens the Oddish, a Bulbasaur appears and fights Starmie while the Oddish runs off into the woods.

title: Charmander - The Stray Pokémon
overview: Our friends, lost, discover an injured Charmander. Pikachu talks to it and learns that it's been waiting a long time for its trainer to return.

title: Here Comes the Squirtle Squad
overview: Ash and his friends walk along a path and fall into a hole dug by a batch of mischievous Squirtle. Ash climbs out and tries to battle them, but they scatter when Officer Jenny arrives.

title: Mystery at the Lighthouse
overview: A tremendously large shadow begins to take form in the sea mists, but this possible discovery could be hindered due to the infamous Team Rocket lurking in the background cooking up another one of their schemes.

title: Electric Shock Showdown
overview: Our heroes finally reach Vermillion City, and they discover that the Pokémon Center is filled with Pokémon hurt during battles with Lt. Surge, the Vermillion City Gym Leader.

title: Battle Aboard the St. Anne
overview: Ash and his friends board a fancy cruise ship called the St. Anne, thanks to tickets provided by two friendly but familiar-looking people. We soon learn that they're actually Jessie and James in disguise!

title: Pokémon Shipwreck
overview: The St. Anne has capsized and sunk with Ash, the gang, and Team Rocket trapped inside. Now, both sides must put aside their differences in order to escape.

title: Island of the Giant Pokémon
overview: As our heroes and Team Rocket beat the odds by surviving the sinking ship, they are dragged into another potentially deadly situation when they are attacked by the ferocious Pokémon, Gyarados.

title: Beauty and the Beach
overview: Ash and his friends have finally arrived in Porta Vista, a beach-side tourist town. After destroying an old man's ship, they must help in the old man's business. To attempt to pay the man back faster, Misty enters a beauty contest, only to discover she is competing against Jessie and James.

title: Tentacool & Tentacruel
overview: Our heroes arrive at Hutber Port, where a ferryboat line runs to the mainland.

title: The Ghost of Maiden's Peak
overview: After leaving Acapulco in search of Saffron City, our heroes finally arrive at a small port town in the middle of celebrating a summer festival.

title: Bye Bye Butterfree
overview: When the party sees a flock of Butterfree flying above the ocean, Brock, as a Pokémon breeder, explains that it is the Butterfree's egg-laying season.

title: Abra and the Psychic Showdown
overview: The party finally arrives in Saffron City where a young girl manages a gym of Pokémon using her psychic powers. If Ash defeats this particular Gym Leader, Ash can earn a gold badge.

title: The Tower of Terror
overview: In search of a ghost Pokémon, Ash heads immediately for a tower in Lavender Town where they are said to dwell.

title: Haunter Versus Kadabra
overview: Unbeknownst to Ash, one of the ghost Pokémon decides to join Ash and friends on their journey and more importantly help Ash battle the powerful Saffron City Gym Leader.

title: Primeape Goes Bananas
overview: On their way to Celadon City, our heroes encounter Mankey, a wild Pokémon that if rubbed the wrong way can go into uncontrollable fits. Not realizing its unpredictability, the party accidentally upsets Mankey.

title: Pokémon Scent-sation!
overview: The party arrives in Celadon City, a perfectly pleasant town full of large department stores, condominiums, and fancy arcades.

title: Hypno's Naptime
overview: Our heroes come to a town in which a great deal of children have recently turned up missing.

title: Pokémon Fashion Flash
overview: At Brock's persistent request, our heroes stop by Caesar Street, a town of breeders famous for its Pokémon beauty salons.

title: The Punchy Pokémon
overview: In the outskirts of Fuchsia City, our heroes come across Hitmonchan, a fighter-Pokémon. With little thought, Ash attempts to capture it, but he and Pikachu are badly defeated.

title: Sparks Fly for Magnemite
overview: As our heroes make their way through the industrial town of Gringy City, Pikachu, perhaps weary from travel, is in a strange state; the electric sacks on his cheeks are continually twitching as though overcharged.

title: Dig Those Diglett!
overview: Ash, Brock, Misty and Pikachu are lost deep in the mountains. As they press forward on a rough road not even shown on their map, they suddenly hear a large explosion.

title: The Ninja-Poké Showdown
overview: Fuchsia Gym, the authorized gym where Ash seeks his next badge, turns out to be a hidden ninja's lair. After finally discovering and entering the gym, he is held up by a complex system of traps.

title: The Flame Pokémon-athon!
overview: Our heroes come to a prairie where the Laramies, a family of Pokémon Ranchers live. They learn from a daughter of the Laramie family that a race is held at the ranch once a year to measure the growth of everyone's Pokémon.

title: The Kangaskhan Kid
overview: Our heroes accompany Officer Jenny into the forest, intent on pursuing the Pokémon poachers in the area.

title: The Legend of Dratini
overview: Ash and his friends get challenged by Team Rocket in seeing who can capture the most Pokémon within the Safari Zone, with Ash catching a whole herd of Tauros. As soon as Ash and his friends head out Pokémon hunting, Team Rocket manages to get the drop on the game warden (Mr. Kaiza) and forces him into giving up the location on where Dratini is (the Dragon Valley). In the end, Ash, his friends, Team Rocket, and Kaiza end up seeing the legendary Dratini and Dragonair, with the heroes saving the day.

title: The Bridge Bike Gang
overview: Ash and the others happen upon a town that can only be reached by a long bike bridge, but they don't have enough money to rent bikes for the crossing.

title: Ditto's Mysterious Mansion
overview: Our heroes take shelter in a cabin that turns out to be the Copycat House of a trainer who has a Pikachu that is a Ditto, the Transform Pokémon.

title: Cyber Soldier Porygon
overview: Ash and his friends arrive at Pokémon Center where the PokéBall transfer machine has been causing problems, as the transferred Pokémon never end up at the other side. Ash and his friends decide to accompany Porygon, a computerized Pokémon, used by Professor Akihabara to investigate the problem, only to discover Team Rocket and another Porygon are to blame.

title: Pikachu's Goodbye
overview: Ash and the others discover a group of wild Pikachu in a secluded area of the woods. How will Pikachu feel seeing others of its kind in the wild?

title: The Battling Eevee Brothers
overview: Tracing a path through the woods, the party comes across a tree baited with a plate of food and water. An Eevee is bound to the trunk with string.

title: Wake Up, Snorlax!
overview: On empty stomachs, our heroes' search for food brings them to a small village, but find there is no food at the bakery nor at the grocery store; even the town restaurant is out of food.

title: Showdown at Dark City
overview: In the middle of a terrible desert, our heroes inadvertently wandered into Dark City. With no name but Dark, the city has fallen into ruin.

title: The March of the Exeggutor Squad
overview: After discovering there's a traveling carnival nearby, Ash, Misty and Brock join in on the fun of the fair and stumble across a rather dull magic show.

title: The Problem with Paras
overview: Team Rocket never let up with their constant interception, but today something seems strange. The coin on Meowth's forehead is blinking red, indicating overheating.

title: The Song of Jigglypuff
overview: Our heroes trudge along, resigning themselves to the idea of camping out in the desert as the sun slowly sets. Suddenly they see bright lights in front of them: a glamorous city appears before them, covered in neon lights.

title: Attack of the Prehistoric Pokémon
overview: Our heroes approach Grampa Canyon, famous for the beautiful scenery of its valley. As they approach, however, they find wave upon wave of people also heading for Grampa Canyon.

title: A Chansey Operation
overview: As our heroes sleep peacefully, Pikachu begins experiencing an unusual sensation. As though it were choking on something, Pikachu's expression grows steadily more and more pained.

title: Holy Matrimony!
overview: As our heroes pass the entrance to a small village, they stop in front of a strange sign.

title: So Near, Yet So Farfetch'd
overview: As Ash sits on a rock, staring at his Pokédex, it displays Farfetch'd, an odd-looking Pokémon holding a leek.

title: Who Gets to Keep Togepi?
overview: When Ash and friends reach a certain Pokémon E. R., Joy informs Ash that a message has arrived for him, from Professor Oak.

title: Bulbasaur's Mysterious Garden
overview: After Bulbasaur's bulb begins to emit an odd glow, a nurse runs some tests and discovers that Bulbasaur isn't sick: It's getting ready to evolve!

title: Princess vs. Princess
overview: Misty and Jessie compete against one another for Queen of the Princess Festival, a day where women rule and the guys have to do whatever the girls tell them.

title: The Purr-fect Hero
overview: It's Kids Day, and Ash and friends help the children at a nearby school celebrate the holiday.

title: The Case of the K-9 Caper!
overview: Ash enrolls Pikachu in a training school for police Growlithe, but the course is tougher than he imagined, for himself and for Pikachu!

title: Pokémon Paparazzi
overview: Ash and friends are being shadowed by Todd Snap, the world's greatest Pokémon photographer.

title: The Ultimate Test
overview: Ash signs up to take an exam to be admitted to the Pokémon League.

title: The Breeding Center Secret
overview: Our heroes discover a terrible secret about the new breeding center in town.

title: Riddle Me This (1)
overview: Ash, Brock and Misty travel to Cinnabar Island where Ash hopes to win his Volcano Badge.

title: Volcanic Panic (2)
overview: Ash continues his quest to attain his Volcano Badge once he finally locates the gym that is suspended above a pool of molten lava!

title: Beach Blank-Out Blastoise
overview: As Ash and the gang are rushing to catch the last ferry off of Cinnabar Island, they stumble over a rare Pokémon in trouble.

title: The Misty Mermaid
overview: One of Misty's water Pokémon is in desperate need of exercise, so the gang decides to take her Horsea to a gym that's run by her sisters in Cerulean City.

title: Clefairy Tales
overview: Ash, Brock and Misty get sidetracked on their way to Viridian City when all of their belongings mysteriously disappear.

title: The Battle of the Badge
overview: Ash and friends finally make their way to Viridian City where Ash hopes to earn his Earth Badge.

title: It's Mr. Mime Time!
overview: On their way back to Pallet Town, Ash, Brock and Misty encounter a runaway Mr. Mime.

title: Holiday Hi-Jynx
overview: Ash, Misty, Brock, and their Pokémon must return a lost Jynx to Santa before Christmas, with the help of a Lapras. Meanwhile, Jessie attempts to take revenge on Santa for stealing her favorite doll when she was young.

title: Snow Way Out!
overview: Pikachu is lost in a snowy mountain forest! Can Ash and the gang stay warm while tracking down their missing pal?

title: Showdown at the Po-ké Corral
overview: Back in Pallet Town, Ash finds Gary, already there, waiting to partake in the Pokémon League. Always having a bitter rivalry with Gary, Professor Oak tells them both the meaning of Pokémon. Meanwhile, Team Rocket is trying to get inside the lab to steal the Pokémon.

title: The Evolution Solution
overview: Ash goes to Seafoam Island to help Professor Oak to discover the mystery of how Slowpoke evolves into Slowbro. There, Professor Westwood V is also trying to solve the mystery. Jessie captures Shellder, and tries to rush a Slowpoke's evolution, forcing Psyduck to stall the evolution.

title: The Pi-Kahuna
overview: Ash and the gang take some time off to go surfing. While surfing, however, Ash passes out, but is thankfully rescued by a surfer named Victor and his blue-eyed Pikachu, Puka. Victor then reveals his plans to surf a tsunami called Humungadunga and become a surfing legend.

title: Make Room for Gloom
overview: Ash goes to a flower garden to get some fertilizer for his mother, but Bulbasaur sniffs the flowers and becomes paralyzed.

title: Lights, Camera, Quack-tion
overview: When the gang discovers there is a movie with an all-Pokémon cast being made nearby, Ash and his friends put their Pokémon in for an audition. Misty's Psyduck gets the main role while the others get minor roles.

title: Go West Young Meowth
overview: Ash and his friends go to Hollywood (although only a small nearby town) to see the premiere of the movie their Pokémon were cast in. Team Rocket follows them to find out that Meowth has history in this town; It is here where he was a member of a gang of street Meowths led by the Persian. He learned to speak and walk upright to win the heart of a female Meowth who was abandoned by her owner. Upon returning, he challenges Persian who was once his mentor for the female Meowth he thought he lost years before.

title: To Master the Onixpected!
overview: After watching Elite Four member Bruno on TV, Ash and his friends decide to do training in the mountains to be as strong as Bruno. However, they meet Bruno and he manipulates them to do some menial tasks for him. Meanwhile, a giant Onix is on a rampage.

title: The Ancient Puzzle of Pokémopolis
overview: While training, Ash and his friends accidentally uncover the ruins of Pokémopolis, an ancient city. After a bit of research, they find a giant Gengar and a giant Alakazam starting to fight. Then, a giant Jigglypuff appears to put them to sleep.

title: Bad to the Bone
overview: Ash and his friends offer to help a trainer that is heading for the Pokémon League to retrieve his stolen gym badges from Team Rocket.

title: All Fired Up!
overview: Ash finally reaches the Indigo Plateau, home of the Pokémon League conference. Before the tournament begins, they plan on lighting a ceremonial cauldron with the flame of the legendary Pokémon, Moltres. Ash, as a participant, is free to carry the flame but when Team Rocket steals it, the tournament may be cancelled.

title: Round One - Begin!
overview: Ash enters his first match of the tournament on the water field against Mandi the Astounding. During the battle against Mandi's Exeggutor, Ash's Krabby evolves into Kingler, who he then uses against Mandy's remaining Seadra and Golbat, winning the match and moving onto the next preliminary round.

title: Fire and Ice
overview: For the second round Ash battles in the rock arena. Ash's last Pokémon, Squirtle, defeats his foe's last Pokémon, Nidorino, and he, victorious, moves on to Round 3. In the third round Ash faces a trainer named Pete in the ice arena. Both trainers are left with 2 Pokémon each. Pete uses Cloyster and Arcanine. In the end, Ash is victorious and he moves on to Round 4.

title: The Fourth Round Rumble
overview: In the 4th round, Ash faces a trainer named Jeanette, who uses a Beedrill, a Scyther, and a Bellsprout. The match ends with Ash victorious, and he moves on to the 5th round, becoming one of the final sixteen members of the tournament.

title: A Friend in Deed
overview: Ash meets a new trainer named Richie, who is eerily similar to Ash, including owning a Pikachu. However, Team Rocket makes another attempt to steal Ash's and Richie's Pokémon. Ash and Richie prevent Team Rocket from stealing their Pokémon. Ash discovers his next battle is against Richie. Will Ash manage to reach the quarterfinals?

title: Friend and Foe Alike
overview: A twist of fate pits Ash and his new friend, Richie, against each other in the fifth round of the Pokémon League Competition. Both are determined to triumph, but who will emerge victorious? And, can their friendship survive the struggle?

title: Friends to the End
overview: After losing to Richie in the fifth round, Ash is moping around, annoyed that he lost. He is convinced by Misty that he should support Richie who is now in the quarterfinals. However, when Richie loses, they both have to look inside themselves to see if they are good trainers.

title: Pallet Party Panic
overview: After battling in the Pokémon League Tournament, Ash and his friends return to Pallet Town for a hero's welcome.

title: A Scare in the Air
overview: Ash, Brock, and Misty prepare for their long journey to Valencia Island to bring back a mysterious Poké Ball for Professor Oak. After discovering that the trip will take a month to get there, Ash manages to win a free blimp ride so it will take only a day! Once on board, they realize the blimp is being powered by Team Rocket! And things only get worse from there!

title: Pokéball Peril
overview: After making a crash landing onto Valencia Island, our heroes get directions to Professor Ivy's laboratory where the mysterious Pokéball is being held. Once at the laboratory, Brock realizes that Professor Ivy and her group of scientists are Pokémon breeders just like him! Suddenly Brock begins to feel at home and thinks about staying for awhile to continue his studies on Pokémon breeding.

title: The Lost Lapras
overview: After picking up the GS Ball from Professor Ivy, Brock decided to stay on the island so Ash and Misty head back home by blimp. Unfortunately, our duo gets double-crossed by Team Rocket once again, and Jigglypuff puts the whole crew to sleep. After another crash landing in the middle of nowhere they meet a new friend named Tracey while trying to help a Lapras that washed up on the beach.

title: Fit To Be Tide
overview: Ash, Misty and their new friend, Tracey, are on a new adventure exploring the Orange Islands as Ash sets out to qualify for the Orange League Tournament. After hearing that the Mikan Gym Leader is known as one of the toughest trainers in the Orange Crew, Ash can't wait for the challenge. He learns that Pokémon battles are run a little differently on the Orange Islands, but he can always rely on his Squirtle to pull them through!

title: Pikachu Re-Volts
overview: Just as Ash, Misty, and Tracey set foot on Mandarin Island, Pikachu and Togepi turn against them and join a group of other angry Pokémon. When the misbehaving Pokémon disappear, Officer Jenny arrives and informs them that dozens of Pokémon have been reported missing, including Meowth! Everyone fears a Psychic attack is behind this mystery. Will Jessie and James actually join forces with our heroes? Could they possibly win a battle for the first time?

title: The Crystal Onix
overview: While riding around the Orange Islands on his Lapras, Ash finds a mysterious message in a bottle, which leads the gang on a new adventure. Ash, Misty and Tracey travel to Sunburst Island, which is famous for its artists and glassblowers. They meet a struggling young shopkeeper who's seeking inspiration from a legendary crystal Onix. Having been unable to find this special Pokémon, the shopkeeper turns to our heroes. With Tracey and his observational Pokémon on board, they set out to fire up a new friend's creative drive!

title: In the Pink
overview: Ash and friends are caught in a whirlpool and swept to an unexplored island where all the Pokémon are pink. It's Pinkan Island, a Pokémon refuge where the Pokémon's diet of Pinkan Berries causes their unique coloration. But the Pokémon aren't all as sweet as they look. Our heroes cross paths with an enraged Nidoking, and end up cornered under a cliff.

title: Shell Shock!
overview: Aboard Lapras, the group follows a news crew to an uninhabited island, where a scientific expedition is underway after the discovery of a fossilized Kabuto. A series of natural disasters place our heroes in mortal danger, with only the power of their Pokémon to help them. What is the secret of the Kabuto, and who wants that secret to remain hidden?

title: Stage Fight!
overview: Misty meets another female trainer who also is an expert on water Pokémon. The new trainer notices some signs that Misty's Psyduck may be ready to evolve. As the two prepare for a Pokémon battle, a Golduck springs from the water and battles for Misty. With her powerful new Pokémon, can Misty defeat both her newfound friend and Team Rocket?

title: Bye Bye Psyduck
overview: After stopping on another island to rest, Misty notices her Psyduck's tail is glowing, a nearby trainer says it may mean it is time for it to evolve. So Misty challenges her to battle and when she finds she has a Golduck, she easily beats the trainer. However, Team Rocket has other plans. Later, Golduck turns out to be a wild Pokémon as Misty's Psyduck makes an appearance.

title: The Joy of Pokémon
overview: While riding Ash's Lapras to the next island, Ash and the gang come across a tanned Nurse Joy who insists on healing Wild Water Pokémon on the island. Ash and Tracey decide to help out, but when Team Rocket arrives to stop them, a giant Magikarp arrives.

title: Navel Maneuvers
overview: The group arrive on Navel Island where Ash finds himself up against the Gym Leader, Danny as Ash competes for his second Orange League badge. Misty tries to tempt Ash by showing more caring attitude to Danny. Ash once again gets jealous and sad.

title: Snack Attack
overview: Arriving on the Grapefruit Islands, Ash discovers that there is a Snorlax going from island to island eating all the grapefruits. Ash is employed to help stop the Snorlax, but the Snorlax proves to be slippery. Later on, Ash captures the Snorlax.

title: A Shipful of Shivers
overview: Arriving on Moro Island, Ash and his friends hear about a long lost Orange League Trophy being found on a sunken ship, but when Team Rocket steals it, Ash and the gang follow them to a ghost ship inhabited by a Gastly and Haunter.

title: Meowth Rules!
overview: Meowth finds himself on an island where the inhabitants believe him to be a "Meowth God".

title: Tracey Gets Bugged
overview: Arriving on Murcott Island, Ash and his friends discover a wounded Scyther. After capturing it, Tracey must take it to a Pokémon Center. While there, he discovers the Scyther got dethroned from being in charge of his group.

title: A Way Off Day Off
overview: Deciding to take a break on a nearby island, Ash, his friends, and all their Pokémon just use the time to relax and get better acquainted. However, Team Rocket has other plans for the Pokémon and intend to steal them.

title: The Mandarin Island Miss Match
overview: The group descend on Mandarin Island where Ash's arrogance emerges due to the fact that he's won every single battle he's had since arriving on the island but upon meeting Prima, a member of the Elite Four and challenging her to a battle, Ash starts to realize that he may have made a grave mistake.

title: Wherefore Art Thou, Pokémon
overview: Still on Mandarin Island, Ash and his friends go through Victorian Town where they find two Nidoran who are in love with each other but any possible hint of a relationship is being derailed by the trainers who happen to be childhood rivals.

title: Get Along, Little Pokémon
overview: Ash and his friends discover a man who has troops of Magnemite and has to take them to a town on the island in order to give it power. However, he gets injured, so it is up to Ash to herd up the Magnemite and get to the town.

title: The Mystery Menace
overview: Heading to the other end of Mandarin Island, Ash and his friends discover there's a monster in the sewer that is causing lots of problems in the town. When the Mayor wants to blow it up, Joy protests and asks Ash and company to help.

title: Misty Meets Her Match
overview: Ash arrives on Trovita Island prepared to battle for his third Orange League Gym Badge. However, much to Ash's dismay, the Gym Leader, Rudy has more than just battling on his mind, Rudy develops a crush on Misty, and Ash gets jealous once again. Rudy moves in to court Misty after she helps rescue his younger sister, Mahri. While Ash battles for the Spike Shell Badge, Misty considers the tempting offer to stay on the island with Rudy and Mahri forever.

title: Bound for Trouble
overview: After getting his 3rd Orange League Badge, Ash and his friends arrive on an island, where Pikachu is soon captured by Team Rocket and is chained to Meowth. But soon after Pikachu and Meowth are taken away by a giant Pidgeot, the trapped rivals eventually learn how to get along.

title: Charizard Chills
overview: After hearing about Ash from the Trovita Island gym leader, another trainer seeks him out for a battle. When Pikachu is knocked out during the battle, Ash turns to his Charizard, but he struggles to make the unruly Pokémon listen to him.

title: The Pokémon Water War
overview: On the next island, Ash and his friends see a team of firefighters consisting of Squirtles, Wartortles, and Blastoises. Eager to test its skills, Ash's Squirtle get to train with them. But when an actual fire turns up, training has to become reality.

title: Pokémon Food Fight!
overview: While in a battle, Ash accidentally breaks Snorlax's Pokéball. With the Pokémon Center on the other side of the island, Ash and his friends have to get Snorlax there. And to make matters worse, Team Rocket arrive and are hoarding the island's food ransom until they get Pikachu.

title: Pokémon Double Trouble
overview: On Kumquat Island, Ash discovers Luana, the final Orange Island Gym Leader, who mistakes him for her son. After defending the island from Team Rocket, they head to the Gym to have a Double Battle where Pikachu and Charizard battle against Marowak and Alakazam but Pikachu's and Charizard's unwillingness to work together might end up costing Ash his chance of qualifying for the Orange League.

title: The Wacky Watcher!
overview: On Rind Island, Tracey meets Dr. Quackenpoker, a noted Pokémon Watcher who is doing a study on the Magikarp that climb up waterfalls and how they evolve into Gyarados. However, Team Rocket appears to steal the Magikarp and get them to evolve.

title: The Stun Spore Detour
overview: On Tiny Island, Ash and Tracey get affected by Stun Spore leaving it up to Misty to get an antidote to heal them. Can Misty find it with the help of her new Poliwag?

title: Hello, Pummelo!
overview: After winning his way to the final competition for the Orange League Winner's Trophy, victory seems a long shot for Ash as the Gym Leader deploys a powerful Gengar that topples one of Ash's Pokémon after another.

title: Enter the Dragonite
overview: As if a gym battle with a Dragonite isn't challenge enough for one day, Team Rocket secretly surveys the weakened state of Ash's battling Pokémon and plots to turn the situation to their advantage.

title: Viva Las Lapras
overview: It's piracy on the open seas and Ash and friends must rescue a local school of Lapras from would-be poachers, but how can they be of help when the Lapras, conditioned to fear humans, shun the young Trainers?

title: The Underground Round-Up
overview: Having to travel by boat, Ash and his friends arrive on Hamlin Island, their final stop before returning to Kanto to discover that the town is being attacked by hundreds of Electrode. A man named Poncho is trying to stop them with his Diglett.

title: A Tent Situation
overview: They’re back in Pallet Town. When they get home, they discover Brock is back and he won’t explain why he left Valencia Island. However, as usual, Team Rocket try to steal all of the Pokémon in Oak’s lab by vacuuming all the Pokéballs, including the GS Ball that Oak was working on. Can they get it back?

title: The Rivalry Revival
overview: An errand of Professor Oak's sets Ash on the road once again, but first he must face his old hometown rival, Gary, in a Pokémon battle.

title: Don't Touch That 'dile
overview: Ash begins his journey in Johto, a largely unexplored region said to be thickly populated with Pokémon entirely new to him and his friends.

title: The Double Trouble Header
overview: In a Pokémon battle, Ash soundly defeats an ambitious young Trainer, who runs away dejectedly.

title: A Sappy Ending
overview: Ash and friends discover a forest where invading Pinsir are causing heavy deforestation.

title: Roll On, Pokémon!
overview: Ash and friends stumble into a small valley where Donphan are used to harvest precious stones.

title: Illusion Confusion!
overview: Who would be foolish enough to brave a thick forest in the dead of night without a Hoothoot guide? Certainly not Ash Ketchum...

title: Flower Power
overview: Our heroes must help prepare an insecure Bellossom dance troupe for their upcoming exhibition.

title: Spinarak Attack
overview: Ash and friends reach a new town and hear of three mysterious robbers apparently mimicking the crimes of a legendary master thief.

title: Snubbull Snobbery
overview: With pet food prepared by a French chef, rooms of WalkMaster exercise equipment and closets stocked with the latest in Pokémon fashion, life couldn't be much better for a Palm Hills Snubbull.

title: The Little Big Horn
overview: Brock saves a lost and frightened Stantler fawn and carefully nurses it back to health before releasing it.

title: The Chikorita Rescue
overview: Ash encounters a wild Chikorita and engages it in battle, thinking to add it to his team.

title: Once in a Blue Moon
overview: Professor Oak's errand—to deliver the mysterious GS Ball to a Poké Ball expert—is interrupted when a Quagsire with a penchant for spherical objects steals the GS ball for use in a nocturnal ritual.

title: The Whistle Stop
overview: When Team Rocket attacks Ash and his friends, a brave young Trainer and her flock of Ledyba come to the rescue.

title: Ignorance Is Blissey
overview: Team Rocket can't believe their luck when the Blissey employed in a Pokémon Center willingly gives them the cente's entire food supply.

title: A Bout With Sprout
overview: Our heroes are recruited as guest instructors at a youth Pokémon Academy.

title: Fighting Flyer With Fire
overview: With Pikachu's help, Ash is confident that winning the Zephyr badge will be a breeze.

title: For Crying Out Loud
overview: Cute and cuddly is one thing, but a bawling Marill is too much even for Misty.

title: Tanks a Lot!
overview: Team Rocket's most recent super vehicle, the Arbotank, is their ticket to world domination.

title: Charizard's Burning Ambitions
overview: When Ash hears of the nearby Charicific Valley, he is sure a quick trip would be just the thing to reignite his Charizard's flagging spirits.

title: Grin to Win!
overview: The annual Sunflora contest is only a day away and yet our hero's new friend and Sunflora Trainer is unable to convince her star Pokémon to compete.

title: Chikorita's Big Upset
overview: A severe case of rivalry between Pikachu and Chikorita calls for therapy sessions with Nurse Joy.

title: Foul Weather Friends
overview: Our heroes chance upon a Hoppip—the fluffy "Cottonweed" Pokémon that is able to drift for miles on a passing wind.

title: The Superhero Secret
overview: When Ash and friends wander into town, they bring with them a challenge that threatens to be Gligarman's last—Team Rocket.

title: Mild 'n Wooly
overview: Team Rocket comes in sheep's clothing to relieve a kind Poké-shepherd of her Mareep flock.

title: Wired for Battle!
overview: When the most promising trainee at a Pokémon dojo abandons battle altogether, it's up to Ash and friends to convince him that battling is more than just a matter of probability.

title: Good 'Quil Hunting
overview: With Charizard gone and no fire Pokémon to call his own, Ash is overjoyed to come across a Cyndaquil in the wild.

title: A Shadow of a Drought
overview: Ash carelessly makes enemies of the Slowpoke-worshipping residents of a town.

title: Going Apricorn!
overview: Ash delivers the mysterious GS Ball to a renowned Pokémon expert, but Team Rocket wreaks trouble when they attempt to harvest the Apricorns essential to local Poké Ball production.

title: Gettin' the Bugs Out
overview: When Ash reaches the Hive Gym, he has just the Pokémon to challenge the Gym Leader's Bug Pokémon.

title: A Farfetch'd Tale
overview: An inexperienced young Trainer mistakenly scares off his own Farfetch'd.

title: Tricks of the Trade
overview: At the Pokémon Swap Meet, our heroes befriend a young Pokémon trainer who's having difficulties finding a trading partner willing to accept his Wobbuffet. When Jessie inadvertently deposits her Poké Ball across from his in the exchanger, this young trainer gets more than he had bargained for.

title: The Fire-ing Squad!
overview: Rocket attempts to steal all the water Pokémon from the annual Fire and Rescue Grand Prix.

title: No Big Woop!
overview: Ash and friends enthusiastically volunteer to watch over a troubled trainer's school of Wooper.

title: Tunnel Vision
overview: While Ash and friends deliberate on how to pass through a tunnel replete with Onix, two wandering Pokémon arrive on the scene together.

title: Hour of the Houndour
overview: Investigating a string of mysterious thefts, our heroes discover a pack of Houndour, struggling to survive in the wild.

title: The Totodile Duel
overview: Ash and Misty's friendly rivalry is tested when they both try to capture a Totodile at the same time. Unable to determine whose Poké Ball had captured the wild Pokémon, the two must battle it out to determine the Totodile's new master.

title: Hot Matches!
overview: When Ash and friends hear of a virtually undefeatable trainer operating nearby, they seek her out.

title: Love, Totodile Style
overview: Ash's Totodile falls head over tail for a passing Azumarill, but is still unsuccessful in having its feelings requited when Team Rocket kidnap the Pokémon before Totodile's eyes.

title: Fowl Play!
overview: Ash has his sights set on a rare and brightly-colored Noctowl.

title: Forest Grumps
overview: When an Ursaring attack leaves Team Rocket and our heroes in compromising positions, compromise they do, members of each team being paired with members of the other.

title: The Psychic Sidekicks!
overview: Our heroes come to a town whose residents all raise Psychic Pokémon to repel the Ghost Pokémon that lurk nearby

title: The Fortune Hunters
overview: A Pokémon Fortune Telling boom turns out to be a Team Rocket plot to steal Pokémon from local trainers.

title: A Goldenrod Opportunity
overview: Arriving in Goldenrod City, Ash is ready for his next Gym battle, but the Gym appears to be closed. Their plans delayed, our heroes decide to explore the vibrant city.

title: A Dairy Tale Ending
overview: After losing to Whitney at the Goldenrod Gym, Ash is thinking of a rematch with her powerful Miltank. When Whitney suggests a trip to a Miltank dairy, it's just the chance he's is looking for!

title: Air Time!
overview: With his new Gym badge in hand, Ash is asked to appear on a local radio station. But while he's acting out a radio drama with some "professional actors," the scripted danger becomes all too lifelike!

title: The Bug Stops Here
overview: A Bug Pokémon Catching Contest turns into a hive of activity when Ash goes up against his rival Casey-and it isn't long before the Team Rocket villains swarm the scene, intent on catching a few things for themselves...

title: Type Casting
overview: On the way to Ecruteak City, our heroes are trapped on the wrong side of a river. To get across, they first have to find a Sudowoodo...which, even for an expert, is no easy feat!

title: Fossil Fools
overview: When an ex-student of Professor Oak's discovers some restored Omanyte and Omastar in the wild, our heroes are called into action to save these prehistoric Pokémon from harsh times with Team Rocket!

title: Carrying On!
overview: The Pidgey Express courier service is in need of our heroes' helping hands, especially when Team Rocket wants to have the delivery Pidgey signed, sealed, and delivered to the Boss!

title: Hassle in the Castle
overview: Caught in a sudden downpour, our heroes find a castle clinic that uses Pokémon healers to help patients. But when Brock and the doctor end up trapped in the castle's labyrinth, a helping hand is needed to get them out!

title: Two Hits and a Miss
overview: After meeting the elderly owner of a Pokémon dojo, Ash is called upon to replace the man's granddaughter as the chosen heir! But when a vicious challenger approaches, can Ash help defend the dojo from destruction?

title: A Hot Water Battle
overview: Chikorita, Cyndaquil, and Totodile find themselves separated from the group and stuck in a ravine! When Meowth finds himself stuck too, an unlikely partnership forms!

title: Hook, Line, and Stinker
overview: Misty is reeled into a rivalry at a local Seaking fishing competition. Can she come out on top?

title: Beauty and the Breeder
overview: A Pokémon breeding contest gives our heroes a chance to compete, greet an old friend, and defeat Team Rocket once again!

title: A Better Pill to Swallow
overview: When Ash comes down with a serious stomach ache far from any hospital, a local healer and his Shuckle companions are on task to set him right!

title: Power Play!
overview: A run-in with his longtime rival Gary leaves Ash in a bad mood. But when Team Rocket's latest weapon starts terrorizing the local Pokémon Center, Ash might have to turn to him for help!

title: Mountain Time
overview: After our heroes meet a mountain patrol and their Ledian partners, they'll have to help protect the mountain from a destructive Team Rocket attack!

title: Wobbu-Palooza!
overview: The peace and quiet of a Wobbuffet festival is interrupted by some hooligans picking a fight. Pokémon battles are banned during the festival, so how can our heroes defend the town?

title: Imitation Confrontation
overview: Ash and the gang are happy to see Duplica again...but not as happy as the Team Rocket trio, when they discover her newest Ditto's tiny transformations!

title: The Trouble with Snubbull
overview: A Snubbull falls into a Team Rocket trap, and the villains try to ransom it back to its wealthy Trainer. But when the Pokémon fights back, Jessie, James, and Meowth might be the ones in trouble!

title: Ariados, Amigos
overview: On their way to Ecruteak City, our heroes must help a familiar Trainer and her Ariados defend a martial arts center from a rampaging Team Rocket!

title: Wings 'N' Things
overview: When a young Trainer's Yanma accidentally destroys his father's precious glasswork, it inspires a Team Rocket scheme that will shatter the peace and quiet of the town!

title: The Grass Route
overview: Hearing of a nearby Grass-type Pokémon tournament, Ash decides to enter...and a rivalry soon begins to bloom amid the heat of battle!

title: The Apple Corp!
overview: Pikachu's in trouble when an orchard owner mistakes it for one of the hungry Pichu that have ransacked her harvests. Ash wants to help, but will the fruits of his labor satisfy both the orchard owner and the Pokémon?

title: Houndoom's Special Delivery
overview: When the smoke clears after their latest run-in with Team Rocket, our heroes quickly realize that Togepi is missing! As they begin the search, Togepi has started making friends in another part of the forest.

title: A Ghost of a Chance
overview: Finally reaching Ecruteak City, our heroes set out to find the Gym, but wind up inside a decaying tower instead. Suddenly, the tower bursts into flames with our heroes trapped inside! How can they escape?

title: From Ghost to Ghost
overview: It is finally time for Ash's fourth Gym battle! After seeing Morty's powerful Gengar in action, Ash is under no illusions about how tough these Ghost-type Pokémon will be! Can he claim the Fog Badge?

title: Trouble's Brewing
overview: When our heroes meet five beautiful sisters in a cultural garden, they are soon challenged to a series of testing battles involving Eevee and some of its evolved forms!

title: All That Glitters!
overview: A mischievous flock of Murkrow cause trouble for Ash and Team Rocket by stealing some of their most prized possessions! Now, the race is on to get them back!

title: The Light Fantastic
overview: In a harsh desert landscape, a strange light appears, and a city of ruins rises up before our heroes. But how did the city come to be this way? Could it have something to do with the beautiful light show?

title: UnBEARable
overview: There's a food thief amongst our heroes...could it have anything to do with the cute and cuddly Teddiursa who has joined them on their journey?

title: Moving Pictures
overview: When our heroes are reunited with Pokémon photographer Todd Snap, he starts them on an adventure to find the Legendary Pokémon Articuno!

title: Spring Fever
overview: Still searching for Articuno, our heroes are found by a trio of keen-nosed, snuffling Swinub, and Team Rocket are keen on sniffing them out for profit!

title: Freeze Frame
overview: Our heroes have finally found Articuno, but they've led Team Rocket right to the Legendary Pokémon as well!

title: The Stolen Stones!
overview: Ash and the gang are rescued by a courier service of Arcanine and Growlithe, and in turn must rescue them and their cargo of Fire Stones from the burning ambition of Team Rocket!

title: The Dunsparce Deception
overview: Our heroes find themselves in a village that adores Dunsparce and try to help one unlucky Trainer find one of his own!

title: The Wayward Wobbuffet
overview: The race is on to find Team Rocket's Wobbuffet when it runs off with the key to an indestructible cage...a cage that holds Ash's Pikachu!

title: Sick Daze
overview: When Brock falls ill, it's up to Ash and Misty to keep the group well and fed. But their inexperience, and the meddling of Team Rocket, makes their new jobs not-so-simple!

title: Ring Masters
overview: Ash finds out about a local Pokémon Sumo competition and decides he wants to enter. He has just the Pokémon for the job: the mighty Snorlax!

title: The Poké Spokesman
overview: A supposed Pokémon translator helps an exhausted Pikachu out on the road, but our heroes soon discover he is wanted by the police for fraud. Whose story do they believe?

title: Control Freak!
overview: Hearing of ancient relics that allow the wearer to control any Pokémon, Jessie, James, and Meowth are preparing to crash an excavation to dig up the treasure of a lifetime!

title: The Art of Pokémon
overview: An aging artist and his Smeargle assistants get in some hot water when they attempt to turn a town into their next great masterpiece!

title: The Heartbreak of Brock
overview: Brock finally meets his match when another Trainer develops a mad crush on him...but with the tables turned, he's not entirely sure how to behave around her!

title: Current Events
overview: After becoming accidentally locked inside an electrical generator, Ash and Chikorita will have to avoid the aggressive security Pokémon and Team Rocket to make it out safely!

title: Turning Over a New Bayleef
overview: Bayleef is still getting used to its newly evolved form, and when Ash becomes impatient, it gets upset and runs away. Can our hero find his friend again and make amends?

title: Doin' What Comes Natu-rally
overview: A young Natu fortune-teller learning his craft is lacking confidence in front of an audience. Can Ash help encourage the young artist's talent?

title: The Big Balloon Blow-Up
overview: Our heroes take part in a high-speed race in a hot air balloon, but the stakes are raised when Team Rocket enters as well, causing chaos in the clouds!

title: The Screen Actor's Guilt
overview: When a hooded man leaves a Smoochum with our heroes, it unravels a tale that will take them all the way to the bright lights of the silver screen!

title: Right On, Rhydon!
overview: Ash and his friends are approached by a civil engineer with a problem: she has a team of Ground-type Pokémon working to build a tunnel, but they're terrified of the water that keeps leaking in! Can our heroes help?

title: The Kecleon Caper
overview: Aboard a luxury blimp, Team Rocket are trying to pinch a few valuables from wealthy travelers-but some courageous Kecleon are on hand to get the better of them!

title: The Joy of Water Pokémon
overview: Misty finally meets one of her idols, a Nurse Joy who specializes in Water types! But the reality doesn't live up to the dream when Nurse Joy reveals that she doesn't share Misty's love for aquatic Pokémon after all...

title: Got Miltank?
overview: Ash and his friends have one last obstacle before Olivine City: a scorching desert! And when Cyndaquil goes missing, they have more than the heat to deal with!

title: Fight for the Light!
overview: Ash's next Gym battle is under way, but it's soon called off by Jasmine, the real Olivine Gym Leader, who claims that Ash has been battling an impostor! Will Ash now get the challenge he's been searching for?

title: Machoke, Machoke Man!
overview: After receiving the medicine in Cianwood City, our heroes soon discover the Gym Leader training hard with his Machoke. It's only a matter of time before Ash decides to go toe to toe with Chuck's Fighting-type Pokémon!

title: Around the Whirlpool
overview: In a scuffle with Team Rocket, Ash and friends are thrown from the ship bearing them to the Whirl Islands! Can they escape the powerful whirlpools that threaten to drag them down?

title: Fly Me to the Moon
overview: The Pidgey on a protected island don't have to worry about being attacked, so most have lost interest in flying. But one of them still dreams of soaring beyond the stars! Can Ash and friends help?

title: Takin' It on the Chinchou
overview: Our heroes pass a small mountain village just in time for the annual Chinchou migration. The village always sends Trainers to protect the Chinchou on their journey-but will they be enough to stop Team Rocket?

title: A Corsola Caper!
overview: When a family's home is threatened by the Corsola who live nearby, it's Ash and friends to the rescue...and maybe Misty can finally catch that Corsola she's been after!

title: Mantine Overboard!
overview: After our heroes miss a ferry, they meet a diver who offers to take them to the next island...but they're soon distracted by tales of treasure aboard a sunken ship!

title: Octillery the Outcast
overview: When they're not competing in the Whirl Cup, Ash and Misty try to help a young Trainer whose Octillery has been rejected by its Remoraid friends after evolving.

title: Dueling Heroes
overview: After winning their preliminary rounds, Ash and Misty face formidable opponents in the Whirl Cup. Meanwhile, the Team Rocket trio have their eyes on stealing the tournament's prize!

title: The Perfect Match!
overview: It's the second round of the Whirl Cup, and Ash and Misty have been paired up against each other! Who will come out on top? And will Team Rocket's plan to distract the police be successful?

title: Plant It Now... Diglett Later
overview: Our heroes are asked to help protect a village from marauders who threaten to make off with the Diglett who live there-and thanks to a delicious dinner, Team Rocket offers to help, too!

title: Hi Ho Silver... Away!
overview: Our heroes meet a jewelry maker who tells them the legend of the fourth Whirl Island...and who soon finds himself embroiled in a Team Rocket plot!

title: The Mystery Is History
overview: While two sets of Team Rocket villains set out to capture the Legendary Pokémon Lugia, Ash runs into his old friend Richie and his partner, a Pikachu named Sparky!

title: A Parent Trapped!
overview: The little Lugia named Silver has fallen into the hands of Butch and Cassidy of Team Rocket, and with Richie's help, Ash must win the adult Lugia's trust before it gets caught in the same trap!

title: A Promise Is a Promise
overview: While trying to rescue the two Lugia from Team Rocket, Ash and friends have been taken prisoner! Now, they must figure out a way to escape...and to free the captured Pokémon as well!

title: Throwing in the Noctowl
overview: Ash and friends are on a plane back to Olivine City when they get caught in a terrible storm that also sweeps up Team Rocket's balloon! They'll have to work together to land safely...but can they manage it?

title: Nerves of Steelix!
overview: After several interruptions, including yet another Team Rocket scheme, Ash's battle against Jasmine at the Olivine Gym is finally getting under way!

title: Bulbasaur... the Ambassador!
overview: When the Pokémon at Professor Oak's lab in Kanto have a hard time getting along, the professor asks Ash to send his Bulbasaur to the lab so it can help keep the peace.

title: Espeon, Not Included
overview: In Ecruteak City, our heroes meet up with their friend Sakura and her newly evolved Espeon. Sakura and her sisters are preparing for a dance competition, but Team Rocket is lurking in the wings, hoping to steal their Pokémon...

title: For Ho-Oh the Bells Toll!
overview: When our heroes investigate a sudden pealing of bells, they discover that the Crystal Bells, which are said to ring only when the Legendary Pokémon Ho-Oh returns, are missing! The researcher Eusine seems to know something...

title: Extreme Pokémon!
overview: When Ash runs into his old rival Gary preparing to enter his Arcanine in a race, he decides to compete as well! But first, he'll have to chase Team Rocket away from the Pokémon Day Care.

title: An EGG-sighting Adventure!
overview: When the Pokémon Egg given to Ash goes missing, our heroes call on the help of a hapless Officer Jenny. It isn't long before they realize that if they want the Egg back, they'll have to find it themselves!

title: Hatching a Plan
overview: The moment has finally come: a Phanpy has hatched from Ash's Pokémon Egg! The little Pokémon is full of energy and soon charging off...with our heroes and Team Rocket in tow!

title: Dues and Don'ts
overview: Jessie, James, and Meowth take time to lick their wounds and dream of stealing Pikachu for the glory of Team Rocket. After a chance meeting with a Team Rocket scout, it seems these dreams are blasting off before their eyes!

title: Just Waiting On a Friend
overview: When thick fog interrupts our heroes' journey, a girl and her Ninetales invite them to rest at a nearby mansion...where Brock and their host fall into a romance that could turn out to be ghostly!

title: A Tyrogue Full of Trouble
overview: Near Mt. Mortar, our heroes run into a Trainer who is trying to curb a Tyrogue's wild ways. But when Team Rocket gets involved, will they be able to save the troublesome Tyrogue from the villains' clutches?

title: Xatu the Future
overview: Our heroes meet a young girl watching over a group of Xatu, whose psychic powers let them predict the future of a local village. When the villagers begin doubting their warnings, can the Xatu prove the truth of their visions?

title: Talkin' 'Bout an Evolution (1)
overview: Reaching the Lake of Rage, Pikachu and Togepi begin to feel sick, and our heroes are attacked by an enraged red Gyarados. When Team Rocket appears on the scene, things look a lot more dangerous for Ash and friends...

title: Rage of Innocence (2)
overview: Things look bad for our heroes, who have been captured by Team Rocket at the Lake of Rage. With the fate of the world's Pokémon at stake, can Ash rise to the occasion and save the day?

title: As Cold as Pryce
overview: Finally reaching Mahogany Town, Ash is warming up for his next Gym battle. But after getting the cold shoulder from Gym Leader Pryce, can our hero convince his frosty opponent to give him a chance?

title: Nice Pryce, Baby!
overview: Pryce and Ash are about to clash in a cold contest in the Mahogany Town Gym. Will Ash be able to show what he and his Pokémon are made of and earn his seventh Johto region Gym badge?

title: Whichever Way the Wind Blows
overview: Our heroes encounter battling groups of Bellossom and Vileplume, who have only recently evolved. As each group fights with increasing number and intensity, can our heroes put an end to the quarrelling?

title: Some Like It Hot
overview: The heat is rising in our heroes' journey when Misty and her Water-type Pokémon go head to head with a willful Fire-type Trainer!

title: Hocus Pokémon
overview: Ash and the gang bump into a Pokémon magician, and soon they're on the hunt to find components for her greatest spell yet!

title: As Clear as Crystal
overview: After yet another close encounter with Team Rocket, Pikachu is left exhausted and weak. Thankfully, a passerby takes our heroes to a nearby lake that helps recharge Electric types...but also hides a great secret!

title: Same Old Song and Dance
overview: Our heroes take time out to enjoy the entertainment of a young Trainer and her Igglybuff variety act. But it's not long before Team Rocket arrives and tries to steal the show!

title: Enlighten Up!
overview: A chance trip to a Slowpoke temple gives our heroes an opportunity to seek enlightenment...and gives Team Rocket the chance to lighten some pockets!

title: Will the Real Oak Please Stand Up?
overview: A lavish celebrity event inspires Jessie and James to pretend to be the VIPs and fill their rumbling tummies. But it's not long before their disguises are put to the test by the real guest of honor: Professor Oak!

title: Wish Upon a Star Shape
overview: When our heroes spot an object falling from the night sky, they rush to investigate. Soon, they find themselves joining forces with a Pokémon mystery club to uncover the secrets shared by the fallen Cleffa and the stars above!

title: Outrageous Fortunes
overview: Ash and Misty get into an argument over who can take on a powerful Trainer and his Poliwrath. When he accepts both challenges, the tough battles that follow give Misty some ideas for her own Poliwhirl!

title: One Trick Phony!
overview: Hearing of a nearby amusement park for battling, Ash is eager to test his skills. But the Team Rocket trio have recently joined the park, and they have a few ideas in store to test this Trainer's mettle!

title: I Politoed Ya So!
overview: It's a case of mistaken identity for Misty's Politoed when it gets roped into replacing the leader of a Pokémon Cheer team...but where has the other Politoed gone?

title: The Ice Cave!
overview: Ash and friends enter an ice cave as it's a shortcut to Blackthorn City. After a watery run-in with Team Rocket, Brock catches a cold and the gang finds a Pokémon Center in the cave. While a Jynx working there is taking care of Brock, Nurse Joy tells Ash and Misty that the cave is cold because of a big building working there as a refrigerator. Team Rocket finds the building and they cause trouble by reversing the structure, heating the cave and cooling the jungle outside, and make their attempt to steal the Ice-type Pokémon as well.

title: Beauty Is Skin Deep
overview: On the outskirts of Blackthorn City, Ash meets Gym Leader Clair, who is busy training her Dratini for a ceremony. But Team Rocket has other plans for the Dragon-type Pokémon, and Ash and Clair must work together to stop them!

title: Fangs for Nothin'
overview: Facing off against Blackthorn Gym Leader Clair, Ash is finding it difficult to overcome the power of her Dragon-type Pokémon. Things are harder still when Team Rocket shows up to steal Blackthorn City's prized Dragon Fang relic!

title: Great Bowls of Fire!
overview: Team Rocket has taken the Blackthorn City Dragon Fang and holed up deep within the Dragon's Den. Things look bad for the ancient relic, but help comes in the form of an old friend!

title: Better Eight Than Never
overview: Ash and Clair finally face off in a Gym battle that will push Ash's skills as a Trainer to the limit! Can he win his final Johto Gym badge?

title: Why? Wynaut!
overview: A Team Rocket trick takes all of Ash's hard-earned Johto Gym badges from him, but it's not long before the troublesome trio get a taste of their own medicine. What tricksy Pokémon could have beaten them at their own game?

title: Just Add Water
overview: Discovering a nearby Pokémon synchronized swim team, Misty goes to check it out, and soon finds herself heading below the surface to take on the team's Trainer in an exciting underwater battle!

title: Lapras of Luxury
overview: Ash barely makes it back to New Bark Town before he is off again, retrieving a Pokémon Egg for Professor Elm. However, things get interesting when he meets an old friend and Team Rocket shows up with more mischief!

title: Hatch Me If You Can
overview: Professor Elm's Pokémon Egg hatches into a Larvitar! The Pokémon is unresponsive even after being checked over at the Pokémon Center, and Ash tries to cheer it up, but soon Team Rocket arrives to ruin all his hard work.

title: Entei at Your Own Risk
overview: On their way to Mt. Silver, our heroes relax at a hot spring and meet a young Trainer who's tracking the Legendary Pokémon Entei. Trouble arises when Team Rocket follows them with a plan to steal Pikachu and Larvitar!

title: A Crowning Achievement
overview: Our heroes find a group of Slowpoke guarding a dried-up stream and hear the tale of a similar drought long ago, when one Slowpoke took the initiative to evolve into Slowking and save everyone. Can the same thing happen here?

title: Here's Lookin' at You, Elekid
overview: A cheeky Elekid starts to bother Larvitar, but soon both Pokémon are in the clutches of Team Rocket. Ash's rival Casey wants to catch Elekid for herself, but can they mount a rescue before the villains get away?

title: You're a Star, Larvitar!
overview: Our heroes have reached Mt. Silver, and Ash wastes no time starting his training for the Johto League Silver Conference. But all the Trainers around provide the perfect prey for Team Rocket, who arrive to swipe all the rare Pokémon!

title: Address Unown!
overview: With Mt. Silver a few yards away, Ash and co. soon spot an Unown, which quickly becomes friends with Larvitar. After which, it was attacked by Team Rocket and soon Ash and friends are swept into another dimension. Where are they now and where is Larvitar? And how did Pikachu and Togepi become giants?

title: Mother of All Battles
overview: After finally arriving at Mt. Silver, Ash and co. find the Preserve where Larvitar was born. There they encounter Larvitar's mother, the same Tyranitar that was in Larvitar's visions. The reunion is not a happy one however, even after they get reunited. The three men from Larvitar's visions were also the ones responsible for stealing its Egg and they managed to capture the Tyranitar. Can they get mother and child reunited with the help of Ranger Mason?

title: Pop Goes the Sneasel
overview: Our heroes are called into action to save the Johto League Silver Conference's opening ceremony from being stopped by a territorial Sneasel!

title: A Claim to Flame!
overview: The Johto League Silver Conference has begun, and Ash and his rivals are getting serious about what the future has in store!

title: Love, Pokémon Style
overview: As the Johto League Silver Conference heats up, Ash will have to rely on some old friends to fight off the affections of his next opponent!

title: Tie One On!
overview: The battle between Ash and Macy is over and Ash wins, but the next battle will determine if he will or will not go to the victory tournament. He faces off against Jackson, a trainer from Twinleaf Town who has a Meganium like Casey. Will Ash be able to win this final battle of the elimination block?

title: The Ties That Bind
overview: It's Ash versus his oldest rival, Gary, in the Johto League Silver Conference, and both Trainers are ready to give it all they've got. But who will come out on top?

title: Can't Beat the Heat!
overview: The ultimate battle continues as Ash and Gary face off. Ash will have to come up with a new plan to beat Gary's Blastoise. Can he do it with the Pokémon he's got, and if Ash beats Gary, who will be his next opponent?

title: Playing with Fire!
overview: After defeating Gary in the last round, Ash has to take on his new friend Harrison in the Johto League Silver Conference. Will our hero be able to stop the challenger from the Hoenn region?

title: Johto Photo Finish
overview: The scorching battle between Ash and Harrison rages on, with Charizard taking on Blaziken, a Pokémon from the Hoenn region!

title: Gotta Catch Ya Later!
overview: With his Johto League adventure behind him, Ash has decided to head back to Pallet Town. But it's all too soon before he has to say goodbye to Brock and Misty, and head out alone for a new adventure!

title: Hoenn Alone!
overview: The Hoenn region is on Ash's mind, and he's preparing to board a ship bound there when Team Rocket strikes! With Pikachu missing and time running out before the boat leaves, can Ash get his adventure back on track?

title: Get the Show on the Road!
overview: Pikachu is suffering from a serious electrical overload! Can Ash and Professor Birch find a solution before it's too late?

title: A Ruin with a View
overview: A power failure at the Pokémon Center leads Ash and his new friend May to an astonishing discovery!

title: There's No Place Like Hoenn
overview: Ash is ready for his first Gym battle in the Hoenn region--and the Gym Leader he must face is none other than May's father!

title: You Never Can Taillow!
overview: When Team Rocket turns a flock of Taillow against our heroes, an old friend comes to the rescue!

title: In the Knicker of Time!
overview: Ash and friends meet an eccentric Trainer who challenges May to her very first Pokémon battle!

title: A Poached Ego!
overview: Our heroes are on the trail of a Pokémon poacher, and Team Rocket is close behind!

title: Tree's a Crowd
overview: When Ash gets lost in the woods, he meets a Treecko who's determined to save its forest home.

title: A Tail with a Twist
overview: Ash's Treecko takes on a wild Seviper, but things don't go so well...

title: Taming of the Shroomish
overview: Stopping in Rinshin Town, our heroes investigate the mystery of a haunted mansion!

title: You Said a Mouthful!
overview: Ash can't resist challenging a Trainer who claims to have the world's most powerful Pokémon, but there's something odd about this battle...

title: A Bite to Remember
overview: After meeting a group of Mightyena, Max befriends a Poochyena who hasn't evolved yet and decides to train it himself!

title: The Lotad Lowdown
overview: Our heroes meet three sisters who grow berries with the help of a nearby group of Lotad!

title: All Things Bright and Beautifly!
overview: Ash and friends discover a whole new kind of Pokémon competition, and it seems May has found her calling!

title: All in a Day's Wurmple
overview: As May sets out to catch a Wurmple for her first Pokémon Contest, Ash receives a Double Battle challenge!

title: Gonna Rule the School!
overview: Our heroes check out the Pokémon Trainer's School in Rustboro City, where Max has a run-in with a classmate!

title: The Winner by a Nosepass!
overview: Armed with Pikachu's new Iron Tail attack, Ash challenges Gym Leader Roxanne!

title: Stairway to Devon
overview: As our heroes tour the massive Devon Corporation, a mysterious intruder tries to steal an important piece of equipment!

title: On a Wingull and a Prayer!
overview: Mr. Briney the sailor offers to take our heroes to Dewford Island for Ash's next Gym battle, but then changes his mind. What's going on?

title: Sharpedo Attack!
overview: When a school of Sharpedo attacks, Ash and friends are stranded on an uncharted island!

title: Brave the Wave
overview: Ash challenges the Dewford Gym, but Gym Leader Brawly seems more interested in surfing!ets the Gym Leader Brawly, who would rather surf than battle, which infuriates Ash. Could there be more to this Gym Leader than meets the eye? And does May even have a shred of decency?

title: Which Wurmple's Which?
overview: May and Jessie of Team Rocket both have a Wurmple, and the two Pokémon accidentally get swapped during a battle!

title: A Hole Lotta Trouble
overview: Ash and friends meet Steven Stone in Granite Cave, where they quickly fall into one of Team Rocket's traps!

title: Gone Corphishin'
overview: A mysterious Pokémon keeps burrowing through the beach near our heroes' camp and knocking over everything in its way!

title: A Corphish Out of Water
overview: When Ash's rowdy new Corphish eats something it shouldn't, he goes on a dangerous journey to get it to the Pokémon Center!

title: A Mudkip Mission
overview: Ash and friends come across a lake full of Mudkip and soon find themselves protecting the Pokémon from a Team Rocket scheme!

title: Turning Over a Nuzleaf
overview: When our heroes get separated from their Pokémon in a deep, dark cave, both groups find themselves in trouble!

title: A Three Team Scheme!
overview: Ash and the others meet up with Professor Birch for a day of exploration and end up in the middle of a villainous confrontation!

title: Seeing Is Believing!
overview: Jessie of Team Rocket is sure her Pokémon will evolve into a lovely Beautifly someday, but she could be in for a rude awakening!

title: Ready, Willing, and Sableye
overview: Deciding it's time to teach his unruly Corphish a lesson, Ash leads the Pokémon into an abandoned mine, where things are not as they seem...

title: A Meditite Fight!
overview: The approach of a dangerous storm calls a halt to Ash's latest battle challenge!

title: Just One of the Geysers
overview: In a secret island arena with an unusual battlefield, Dewford Gym Leader Brawly welcomes Ash for a rematch!

title: Abandon Ship!
overview: Our heroes find themselves aboard an abandoned ship...and then start disappearing one by one!

title: Now That's Flower Power!
overview: While May trains for the Pokémon Contest in Slateport City, Torchic's appetite for berries gets her in trouble!

title: Having a Wailord of a Time
overview: A new Trainer is stopping by a Pokémon Center to choose her first partner Pokémon, and Nurse Joy asks Ash and Max to take care of Treecko, Torchic, and Mudkip for her!

title: Win, Lose or Drew!
overview: It's time for May's first Pokémon Contest! Does her Beautifly have what it takes to win a ribbon?

title: The Spheal of Approval
overview: A day of relaxation in Slateport City turns into a confrontation between Team Rocket and Team Magma!

title: Jump for Joy!
overview: Ash and friends stop at a Pokémon Center for directions, but there's no sign of Nurse Joy! What could have happened to her?

title: A Different Kind of Misty!
overview: Ash and friends visit a lighthouse, where they try to return a stolen item they recovered from Team Rocket--but the lighthouse keepers think our heroes are the thieves!

title: A Poké-BLOCK Party
overview: Ash and friends join the Trick House Contest, which sends them through a maze full of tricks and traps--and, of course, Team Rocket!

title: Watt's with Wattson
overview: When Ash challenges the Mauville City Gym, he soon finds himself face to face with the Legendary Pokémon Raikou, or so it would seem...

title: What You Seed Is What You Get
overview: When Ash's Treecko and Corphish face Grovyle and Slugma in a double battle, Treecko turns the tables, learning to use Bullet Seed.

title: Love at First Flight
overview: Our heroes discover a love story between two Trainers who star with their Pokémon in a local dance festival.

title: Let Bagons Be Bagons
overview: A Trainer learns that Pokémon battling requires more than tactics and technology. Meanwhile, a Bagon dreams the impossible dream of taking flight.

title: The Princess and the Togepi (1)
overview: An invitation to the Togepi Festival brings Misty to the Hoenn region, where she learns that a local kingdom bases its rule on the whims of passing Togepi.

title: A Togepi Mirage! (2)
overview: Our heroes go up against an evil regent who would use Misty's Togepi to steal the throne from its rightful heir!

title: Candid Camerupt!
overview: Ash, Brock, May, and Max must all work together to face the four members of a family of skilled Pokémon Trainers and their powerful Pokémon.

title: I Feel Skitty!
overview: After her Skitty is exhausted by chasing its own tale, May entrusts it to a local aromatherapy house, where it recovers completely.

title: ZigZag Zangoose!
overview: A young trainer gains confidence and strengthens his bond with his Zangoose, when he is forced to face Seviper in a less than fair battle.

title: Maxxed Out!
overview: Max meets another young boy who shares his name, but differs greatly in personality. When this young and inexperienced new acquaintance goes off on his own, Ash and friends must track him down.

title: Pros and Con Artists (1)
overview: As May prepares for her first contest, Ash faces another contestant in a pre-contest battle.

title: Come What May! (2)
overview: May wins her first contest, finally earning a ribbon. Jessie of Team Rocket attempts to win by cheating, but is disqualified.

title: Cheer Pressure
overview: Ash and friends meet an old friend who has since joined a Pokémon cheering squad. When they expose his squad for a fake, the trainer is inspired to start anew.

title: Game Winning Assist
overview: When Team Rocket threatens an innocent family of ranchers, Ash and friends drive them away, with May's Eneko using Assist for the first time.

title: Fight for the Meteorite
overview: When Team Aqua and Team Magma battle to gain a meteor on Mount chimney, Ash and friends must put an end to both of their dastardly plans.

title: Poetry Commotion!
overview: No sooner has Ash begun an official battle with a novice gym leader, Flannery, than Team Rocket interrupts, postponing the battle for an extra day.

title: Going, Going, Yawn
overview: Ash is battling for his fourth Hoenn region Gym badge! Can his Corphish defeat Flannery's team of Fire types?

title: Going for a Spinda
overview: When rumors arise that a particular Spinda draws happiness to its trainer, Team Rocket quickly hatches a plan to steal this lucky Pokémon.

title: All Torkoal, No Play
overview: Ash and friends struggle to pass through a valley guarded by territorial Steel Pokémon.

title: Manectric Charge
overview: While Ash uses his Torkoal to battle the powerful Watson, May faces his assistant with her Skitty.

title: Delcatty Got Your Tongue
overview: When May's Skitty is injured far from a Pokémon Center, a master in the art of Pokémon massage comes to the rescue!

title: Disaster of Disguise
overview: When Ash and friends learn that a masked coordinator is attempting to compete in secret, they help the timid boy hide his identity.

title: Disguise Da Limit
overview: At Vendanturf Town, the masked trainer makes a strong performance, but loses to May's Skitty, as May wins her second ribbon.

title: Take the Lombre Home
overview: The people of a small village blame a local drought on the Pokémon Solrock, but it soon comes to light that the drought is in fact the work of Team Rocket.

title: True Blue Swablu
overview: May feels a special duty to help a wounded Swablu return to its flock.

title: Gulpin It Down!
overview: Mayhem ensues when Professor Oak inadvertently creates a gigantic Gulpin and Treecko in an effort to stop a Gulpin outbreak.

title: Exploud and Clear
overview: Even as Ash's Treecko evolves in the course of battle, his opponent's Loudred does the same. Team Rocket couldn't have picked a worse time to attack.

title: Go Go Ludicolo!
overview: Ash and friends must rely on Grovyle, Torkoal, and Corphish, as they approach Petalburg City.

title: A Double Dilemma
overview: When May and Max's father, Norman, reaches near celebrity status, Team Rocket pretends to be a part of the family.

title: Love, Petalburg Style!
overview: Rumors are spreading about Nurse Joy spending a little too much time with Norman, Max and May's father! What's going on?

title: Balance of Power
overview: Ash has challenged Max and May's father, Norman, to a battle before—but this time, it's for a Gym badge!

title: A Six Pack Attack!
overview: When Professor Oak makes a surprise appearance in Hoenn, bringing along Bulbasaur, Charmander, and Squirtle, Ash joins forces with them to battle Team Rocket.

title: The Bicker the Better
overview: When Ash and May face a duo in a double Pokémon battle, a falling out between these two friends causes them to lose--but they soon resolve their differences.

title: Grass Hysteria!
overview: When May accidentally enters a forbidden spot where only grass Pokémon live, she quickly befriends a friendly Bulbasaur.

title: Hokey Pokéballs
overview: May lets her Bulbasaur out of its Pokéball and the curious Pokemon soon gets into trouble.

title: Whiscash and Ash
overview: When a famed Whiscash swallows Ash's Gym badges, he teams up with a fisherman to try to reel it in!

title: Me, Myself and Time
overview: Ash and Pikachu meet up with an aspiring archaeologist who leads them to an ancient and mystical site.

title: A Fan with a Plan
overview: May prepares to enter her first contest open only to ribbon holders, but remains confident despite seeing the contest favorite and his powerful Masquerain.

title: Cruisin' for a Losin?
overview: May decides to use Bulbasaur in the Rubello Town Contest. Will its lack of experience cost her a ribbon?

title: Pearls Are a Spoink's Best Friend
overview: When a Spoink loses the pearl on its head, Ash and friends volunteer to help search for it.

title: That's Just Swellow
overview: Ash, May, and Brock participate in a curious sporting event in which players use rings dangling from balloons to catch balls lying on the ground.

title: Take This House and Shuppet
overview: Fights arise both between May and Max and amongst Team Rocket, as a Shuppet--a Pokémon that feeds on human anger--stirs them to conflict.

title: A Shroomish Skirmish
overview: Team Rocket infuriates a Breloom, forcing not only themselves, but also Ash and friends to flee from its wrath.

title: Unfair-Weather Friends
overview: Ash and friends learn of Groudon and Kyogre, when Team Magma is joined by a master of disguise.

title: Who's Flying Now?
overview: Ash and friends meet up with the beautiful gym leader, Winona, at the Feather Carnival.

title: Sky High Gym Battle!
overview: It's Swellow versus Swellow as Ash and Winona battle it out in the Fortree City Gym!

title: Lights, Camerupt, Action!
overview: As the heroes continue onwards, they encounter a man pushing his Camerupt. After helping the man, the heroes are told the man, named Elijah, goes around villages and shows movies. The heroes decide to accompany them, while Team Rocket plans on stealing his equipment.

title: Crazy As a Lunatone
overview: A group of conspiracy theorists known as the Pokemon Mystery Club pursues a Lunatone suspected to be behind a recent series of strange events.

title: The Garden of Eatin'
overview: A troubled Snorlax wanders into a public park, creating no end of trouble.

title: A Scare to Remember
overview: A fierce bout of amnesia causes Pikachu to join forces with Team Rocket!

title: Pokéblock, Stock, and Berry (1)
overview: Before taking part in the Lilycove Coordinator Contest, May has time to study the art of Pokéblock preparation with a local master

title: Lessons in Lilycove (2)
overview: The Lilycove Coordinator Contest is underway but things are not looking good for May--her Combusken's Fire Spin has spiraled out of control!

title: Judgment Day!
overview: Hoping to get to Mossdeep Island, Ash and the gang are forced to take a detour.

title: Clamperl of Wisdom
overview: Our heroes reconnect with an unlucky Spoink, and it's still having trouble holding onto its pearl!

title: The Relicanth Really Can
overview: Ash and friends join a treasure hunter to explore the deep sea, but Team Rocket is right behind them!

title: The Evolutionary War
overview: When a Gorebyss-versus-Huntail rivalry develops between two islands, our heroes must get to the bottom of it!

title: Training Wrecks
overview: The residents of Muscle Island are dedicated to physical fitness-but can they keep up with Ash in a Pokémon battle?

title: Gaining Groudon (1)
overview: After a rescue at sea, our heroes find themselves aboard Team Magma's submarine and learn more about the group's plan for world domination!

title: The Scuffle of Legends (2)
overview: Team Aqua and Team Magma have awakened the ancient Legendary Pokémon Groudon and Kyogre! Can Ash and friends escape?

title: It's Still Rocket Roll to Me
overview: A Team Rocket strike on Mossdeep City drags our heroes-plus the city's twin Gym Leaders-into a space-shuttle scheme!

title: Solid As a Solrock
overview: Ash takes on the Mossdeep Gym, going up against two Gym Leaders at once!

title: Vanity Affair
overview: After winning his seventh Gym badge, Ash can't stop bragging about his battle skills...until he runs into a member of the Elite Four!

title: Where's Armaldo?
overview: In an island nature preserve, Ash and friends come face to face with several Pokémon thought to be extinct!

title: A Cacturne for the Worse
overview: May competes in a Pokémon Contest, where another Coordinator tries to sabotage her performance!

title: Claydol Big and Tall
overview: When our heroes discover an enormous Poké Ball on an island, Team Rocket's meddling unleashes an ancient destructive force!

title: Once in a Mawile
